history.content:
<thought>
The goal (G) is visible to my right and slightly ahead. The cell directly in front of me is lava (L), so moving forward would be deadly. I will turn right to face toward the safer path leading around the lava toward G.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal (G) is directly in front of me, but the immediate cell in front is lava (L). Since moving forward would be fatal, I need to avoid lava by turning toward the open floor leading around it toward G.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a wall to my left and a wall just beyond; the only safe-looking option is to move forward into the open floor, since lava is not visible in my current view. I will step forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The local map still shows only the goal and lava are not visible; forward appears to be an open floor cell (no wall or lava directly ahead). I should move forward to progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation does not appear to have changed: I’m still boxed in by walls (#) and my position marker is unchanged. Since forward seems to keep me from making progress, I should rotate to search for an opening.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can now see open floor cells to my right (.) and the goal is not yet visible. The cell in front is also floor, not a wall or lava, so moving forward should allow me to exit the corner and explore safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The map still shows my position unchanged at the same spot, so MOVE_FORWARD likely was blocked by a wall. I should turn to find a direction where the forward cell is not a wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I rotated and now my front cell appears to be a floor '.' rather than a wall '#', so I can move forward to advance into the open area while avoiding lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
After attempting to move forward, the view did not change, which suggests I was still blocked by a wall. I should turn to look for an open cell to move into.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see floor cells ahead ('.') and no lava 'L' or wall '#', and there is open space near the bottom row. I will move forward into the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is still blocked (my position appears unchanged and the wall boundary remains), so moving forward is not possible. I need to change my facing direction to find a path around the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can now see that I’m next to open floor on my left ('.') and a wall on the right ('#'). Since moving forward might hit a wall, I will turn left to face the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal 'G' is visible to the left of my current position, and the cell in front appears to be free '.' (not lava 'L' or wall '#'). To approach the goal, I will move forward into the open cell.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing toward the interior corridor; the cell in front is a lava cell 'L' (shown immediately ahead). To avoid lava, I should turn right to face a safe direction rather than moving forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal 'G' to my left in the corridor, but the cell I’m currently facing is a wall '#', so moving forward would fail. I should turn left to align with the open path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>
